original_hypothesis.title: Missing quitLock Function Leaves Users Without Early Exit Mechanism for 5-Year Locks
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The LockAction enum defines QUIT (implying an early exit with penalty, standard in Curve-derived implementations), but no quitLock() function exists. Users who lock CANTO are committed for 5 years with every action (createLock, increaseAmount) resetting the lock duration. The only escape is the governance-controlled unlockOverride, which is a global all-or-nothing toggle. Users have no individual early exit mechanism, even with a penalty, and governance must risk the voting power amplification bug (OPS-H007) to provide any relief.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The absence of an individual early exit mechanism combined with the 5-year lock that resets on every action creates a significant user safety concern. If unlockOverride is the only relief valve, it introduces systemic risk.

proof_of_signal.pattern: LockAction.QUIT defined in enum at line 63 but no quitLock() function exists; withdraw requires lock.end <= block.timestamp || unlockOverride

preconditions: User has an active lock with CANTO

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: QUIT enum variant exists without corresponding function; user funds locked for full 5-year duration
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Only governance can enable early withdrawal via unlockOverride

operational_questions: Was quitLock intentionally omitted or accidentally left out?
What penalty rate would be appropriate for early exit?
How does the lack of individual exit affect user adoption?
validation_requirements: Verify no early exit path exists besides unlockOverride
Verify increaseAmount resets lock to full 5 years

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Financial circumstances change, user needs liquidity
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: User must wait full 5 years or lobby governance for unlockOverride

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: User funds locked for extended periods without recourse
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Only governance intervention via unlockOverride (which has side effects per OPS-H007)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
